feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Getting error when i am trying to build mbt
How to Emphasize or add Color Coding to Custom Buttons in Fiori Elements created via Controller Ext.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have created a Fiori Elements Application with the List & Object Page. In the Object Page, we have created two Buttons in header using Controller extension with Page map as shown below.

By default those buttons are not Emphasized or can't add any color for button as shown below.

 

How can we achieve this to Emphasize the button or add some Color as UI Guidelines?

Thanks for feedback

SAP Fiori MakersSAP Fiori Elements

entry.title: HOW to securing odata services or api in sap btp CAPM in node js
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m currently working on a client project with my team using the SAP BTP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) in Node.js, with CDS for our backend services. We have successfully created OData services and APIs, but we’re facing challenges in securing them effectively.

In other technology stacks like .NET and MERN, the standard approach we follow for securing APIs typically involves:

  1. Verifying the user based on their login credentials against the data stored in the database.
  2. Generating a token upon successful validation.
  3. Using authentication middleware to secure the APIs.

However, we are unsure how to apply this approach in the context of SAP BTP CAP. The usual methods of user authentication, token generation, and middleware usage seem different or not directly applicable.

Could you please guide us on the best practices for securing OData services or APIs in CAP? Any recommendations, frameworks, or configurations specific to SAP BTP CAP would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Problems with AppState in FIORI-App on Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Hello,

we have a CAP Application with a FIORI-App running on BTP (CloudFoundry).

The FIORI-App is a List-Report Page with some custom extensions. The share-Button works on Detail-/Object Page and at Search-Page it doesn´t work properly. The filter criterias where not shared. I think it has to do with an error i can see in console of the browser.
TypeError: s.storeInnerAppStateAsync is not a function.

Have someone this error too, and can someone give me a solution for it?
Maybe it's also related to the fact that the app doesn't run in FLP?

entry.title: Client Error: IF_HTTP_CLIENT object caused HTTP receive error: HTTP_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 I have created a custom class and i am calling the API of Maintenance order operation to get the operation data when i use GET Method  its working when i use POST Method its giving me below error . Its not fetching the token while POST. 

Client Error: IF_HTTP_CLIENT object caused HTTP receive error: HTTP_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE

if anyone faced the issue before can you please help and guide.
Thanks for your help
Nitin

entry.title: Cloud Application Programming Model OData v2 proxy
entry.content:
I am running a cap backend application with express.js and want to access the odata services with v2. In this project I used the @sap/cds-odata-v2-adapter-proxy which is now deprecated. The recommended package is now @sap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter.

I tried to implement the proxy in the same way as the deprecated proxy. It did not work and I did not get any error messages.

This is my server.js:

 

const express = require('express');
const proxy = require('@sap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter');
const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const app = express();
const bodyParser = require('body-parser');

app.use(proxy());
app.use(bodyParser.json());

(async () => {
  await cds.serve('TestService').in(app).at('/test');

  app.listen(process.env.PORT || 4004);
})();

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements and SAP CAP: How to make a field editable depending on condition in Object Page
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a SAP CAP application using Fiori elements for UI, and Im struggling trying to make a field editable on condition in my annotations.cds.

I have this field, and I would like to make it editable depending on status, which is a field from the same entity:

    UI.FieldGroup #1 : { 
        $Type : 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [         
           {
                $Type : 'UI.DataField',
                Value : description,
            },
        ]
   }

 The only thing I found is adding this sentence: 

![@Common.FieldControl]: #ReadOnly
 
But it makes it non editable, so Im trying to make an edm.json condition for the readonly annotation, but not working. 
Is there are any workaround or new approach for this? I think this should be easier to do, so maybe Im focusing bad.
Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Create CAP CDS entity via synonyms
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I have 3 HANA based Calculation views in sys_bic schema.The synonyms created for the views.

How to create proxy entity based on synonyms?

entry.title: SAP CAP: Is there any way to modify UI annotations via nodejs events in CAP?
entry.content:
I want to modify/add the readonly property of some fields based on another table to make them editable/non-editable.

I tried with approaches like:

req.target.elements.description["@readonly"] = true
req.target.elements.description._isReadOnly = true...
 
But none of them working. Also tried to put them in the .before, .on and .after and no luck.
Is it possible to do this like im doing? Or I should change the approach?

entry.title: How to enable parallel processing on dwc concurrent calls to CAP service
entry.content:
Trying to make concurrent calls to CAP service using DWC and it is throwing "Caused by: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Could not determine dwc landscape base route"

 

StackTrace:

java.lang.IllegalStateException: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Could not determine dwc landscape base route,
at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance0(Native Method),
at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance(Unknown Source),
at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance(Unknown Source),
at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Constructor.newInstanceWithCaller(Unknown Source),
at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Constructor.newInstance(Unknown Source),
at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinTask.getThrowableException(Unknown Source),
at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinTask.reportException(Unknown Source),
at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinTask.invoke(Unknown Source),
at java.base/java.util.stream.ReduceOps$ReduceOp.evaluateParallel(Unknown Source),
at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.evaluate(Unknown Source),
at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.collect(Unknown Source),

entry.title: How to enable parallel processing on dwc concurrent calls to CAP service
entry.content:
Trying to make concurrent calls to CAP service using DWC and it is throwing "Caused by: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Could not determine dwc landscape base route"

java.lang.IllegalStateException: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Could not determine dwc landscape base route,
at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance0(Native Method),
at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance(Unknown Source),
at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance(Unknown Source),

entry.title: SAP CAP Attachment Plugin
entry.content:
Hi Developers,

I would like to inquire whether the SAP CAP Attachment plugin supports multi-tenancy. Additionally, are there any limitations regarding file size and the ability to upload multiple files in an SAP CAP Node.js application?

Regards,

Syed

entry.title: Problem in the deployment of the CAP project
entry.content:
Hello experts, I hope you are well.

I am currently performing my first deploy to hana database and I am encountering a mapping error but according to me, everything looks correct.

 

 

 

 

Error: com.sap.hana.di.tabledata: A column mapping "DESCRIPTION" : "Description" is specified, but the column "Description" cannot be found in the CSV file "com.logali.materials-Currencies.csv" [8251506]
at "src/gen/data/com.logali.materials-Currencies.hdbtabledata" (21:26,/imports[0]/column_mappings)
Error: com.sap.hana.di.tabledata: Could not parse the data mappings [8251524]
at "src/gen/data/com.logali.materials-Currencies.hdbtabledata" (0:0)
Error: com.sap.hana.di.tabledata: Deploying "src/gen/data/com.logali.materials-Currencies.hdbtabledata$0.expand"... failed [8212145]
at "src/gen/data/com.logali.materials-Currencies.hdbtabledata$0.expand" (0:0)

 

 

 

 


Entity declaration Currencies:

 

 

 

 

context materials {
    entity Currencies {
        key ID          : String(3);
            Description : localized String;
    }
}

 

 

 

 

File com.logali.materials-Currencies.csv:

 

 

 

 

ID,Description
USD,Dollar
EUR,Euro

 

 

 

 

and file com.logali.materials-Currencies_texts

 

 

 

 

locale,ID,Description
en,USD,Dollar
es,USD,Dolar
en,USD,EuroEN
es,USD,EuroES

 

 

 

 

com.logali.materials-Currencies.hdbtabledata: ( 8251506 A column mapping "DESCRIPTION" : "Description" is specified, but the column "Description" cannot be found in the CSV file "com.logali.materials-Currencies.csv"HDI Deploy(/imports[0]/column_mappings) )

 

 

 

{
  "format_version": 1,
  "imports": [
    {
      "target_table": "COM_LOGALI_MATERIALS_CURRENCIES",
      "source_data": {
        "data_type": "CSV",
        "file_name": "com.logali.materials-Currencies.csv",
        "has_header": true,
        "type_config": {
          "delimiter": ","
        }
      },
      "import_settings": {
        "import_columns": [
          "ID",
          "DESCRIPTION"
        ],
        "include_filter": []
      },
      "column_mappings": {
        "ID": "ID",
        "DESCRIPTION": "Description"
      }
    }
  ]
}

entry.title: Lack of understanding or missing documentation in the area of SAP CAP & Fiori Elements
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm currently struggling with a simple application I'm building using CAP and Fiori Elements. However, I'm unsure if this is due to my lack of understanding or missing documentation in the area.


What I'm trying to do is to build a simple app for creating orders. The orders consist of a header and items. Here is the excerpt of the schema.cds:

 

 

entity Orders : cuid, managed {
    orderID     : Integer @readonly default 0;
    customer    : Association to one Customers not null;
    orderDate   : Date not null;
    orderAmount : Decimal(10, 2);
    currency    : Currency;
    orderStatus : Association to OrderStatus default 10;
    items       : Composition of many OrderItems
                      on items.order = $self;
}

entity OrderItems : cuid, managed {
    itemID     : Integer @readonly default 0;
    order      : Association to Orders;
    product    : Association to one Products;
    quantity   : Integer;
    itemAmount : Decimal(10, 2);
    currency   : Currency;
}

 

 

The idea is to have a semantic key (orderID and itemID) in the orders and the items. On the basis of the schema.cds I created the following service.cds. The main point is the annotation with @odata.draft.enabled to enable the creation of the entities in the Fiori elements app.

 

 

service SimpleERPService {
    entity Products as projection on erp.Products;
    entity Customers as projection on erp.Customers;
    entity Orders as projection on erp.Orders;
    entity OrderItems as projection on erp.OrderItems
}

annotate erp.Products with @odata.draft.enabled;
annotate erp.Customers with @odata.draft.enabled;
annotate erp.Orders with @odata.draft.enabled;

 

 

 What I tried to implement first was an automatic generation of the itemID. What I came up with after:

  • lots of debugging
  • lots of trial and error
  • looking at these samples:
    • https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-cap-samples 
    • https://github.com/SAP-samples/fiori-elements-v4-cap-advanced/
    • https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-sflight 

is this code in service.js:

 

 

class SimpleERPService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  init() {
    const { Orders, OrderItems, Products } = this.entities;
...
    this.before("UPDATE", "OrderItems.drafts", async (req) => {
      const itemDraft = await SELECT.one
        .from(req.query.target)
        .where({ ID: req.data.ID });

      if (!itemDraft.itemID) {
        const { maxID } = await SELECT.one("max(itemID) as maxID")
          .from(req.query.target)
          .where({ order_ID: itemDraft.order_ID });
        req.data.itemID = maxID + 10;
      }
      //additional code to calculate itemAmount
      ...
    });

 

 

While the code above works, there are a few things I don't quite understand. Or that in my opinion contradict the documentation at https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/

  1. OrderItems.draft is always undefinded.
    In contrast to the documentation here (https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/advanced/fiori#query-drafts-programmatically) OrderItems.draft is always undefinded. I have to use the String "OrderItems.draft" to register the handler. What is the reason for this?
  2. Selection from draft tables.
    As the *.draft is allways undefined (see above) the approach to read data from drafts shown in the samples (e.g. https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-sflight/blob/4fc76dc0ae7ad2d0dd6c97afd5c143c62fc93c4d/srv/travel-service.js#L28) does not work. Instead I can use req.query.target. However, req.data.target does not  work in all cases. For example, if  I need to update the draft of an other entity I have to use the full name of the draft table as shown below. This does not seem quite right. Is there a better way to do this?

 

return UPDATE("SimpleERPService_Orders_drafts", ID).with({
        orderAmount,
        currency_code,
      });​

 

  • PATCH event can't be registered.
    The PATCH event mention in the docu (https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/core-services#srv-on-before-after) and in the samples (https://github.com/SAP-samples/fiori-elements-v4-cap-advanced/blob/89e073020b208ac2121718cfb8aac5586999bab6/srv/travel-service.js#L50) is never raised in my case. I have to use UPDATE. What is the reason for this?

Thanks in advance for the help!
Christian

entry.title: Issue when exposing Succesfactor API through CAPM
entry.content:
Im Following the Developer Tutorial https://developers.sap.com/mission.btp-extend-sfsf-cap.html

and I had followed all the code and Destination as per the Tutorial.

Have imported the edmx 

package.json

lib/handler.js : 

Service.cds 

Getting the error when try to hit the GET on 

@ale_biagi Help us to understand if we are missing something  we are just Reading the Data and not doing any Create or Update. We are not able to Read the SF API from CAP as mentioned in tutorial https://developers.sap.com/mission.btp-extend-sfsf-cap.html

and Community blog 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/extend-sap-successfactors-on-sap-btp-with-cap-add-business-logic/ba-p/13526502

Thanks 

Sahasranaman

entry.title: Query to create draft for a child entity in composition
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am using CAP(Node.js) odata V4 & listReport page template with fiori elements. I have two entities Lets say parent & child, they have a composition relation between them. The child entity table is displayed on the object page and there i have added a custom action button which opens a valuehelp dialog and there user can add multiple entries from the value valuehelp. The reason custom button was added was that it enables the user to add multiple entries in one go and he doesnt have to create it one by one using the create button. When you try to create an entry in child table using create button the call that is sent is this : 

NEW Parent(ID=123,IsActiveEntity=false)/child
i want to replicate this call. I tried using the following but it didnt work :
await this.send({
            query: INSERT.into(`Parent(ID=${req.params[0].ID},IsActiveEntity=false)/child`).entries(data),
            event: "NEW ",
        });
Any ideas how to solve this ?

entry.title: Business process automation form failure
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing this issue in Business process automation form. After the workflow is getting approved by a user, the instance goes into the following error:  Failed to build headers. The destination tried to provide authorization tokens but failed in all cases. This is most likely due to misconfiguration. Original error messages: Retrieval of OAuthToken failed due to: Unable to fetch refresh token from the specified token service URL. Response was Token header claim [kid] references unknown signing key :

entry.title: 415 unsupported Mediatype issue in CAP
entry.content:
UI CODE :

 

 

_genericPostUploadFile: function (url, fileData, token, that, successCallback, errorCallback) {
					const formData = new FormData();
					formData.append('file', fileData);
					jQuery.ajax({
						type: "POST",
						url: url,
						processData: false,
                        contentType: false,
						data: formData,
						beforeSend: function (xhr) {
							xhr.setRequestHeader("X-CSRF-Token", token);
						},
						success: function (result) {
							if (successCallback) {
								successCallback(that, result);
							}

						},
						error: function (e) {
							if (errorCallback) {
								errorCallback(that, e);
							}
						}
					});
			

			},

 

 
 
CAP CODE :

 

    action uploadLogFilePr()   returns String;

excel.cds​

 

excel.js

 

 this.on('uploadLogFile', async (req) => {

        const { uploadLogFile } = require('./api/excel_v2/generalFunctionsExcelPr');


        let result;
        let file;
        let parameters = {};

        file = req.data
        parameters.FILENAME = req._queryOptions.FILENAME
        parameters.USER = req._queryOptions.USER
        parameters.DATE = req._queryOptions.UPLOAD_DATE
        parameters.TYPE = req._queryOptions.TYPE


        result = await uploadLogFile(file, parameters)

        return result

    })

 

error :

 

{
    "error": {
        "code": "415",
        "message": {
            "lang": "en",
            "value": "Unsupported Media Type"
        },
        "severity": "error",
        "target": "/#TRANSIENT#",
        "innererror": {
            "errordetails": [
                {
                    "code": "415",
                    "message": {
                        "lang": "en",
                        "value": "Unsupported Media Type"
                    },
                    "severity": "error",
                    "target": "/#TRANSIENT#"
                }
            ]
        }
    }
}

 

i am getting file from UI5 in formdata, as binary but CAP is throwing 415 error (unsupported mediatype) how to fix this issues @gregorw @SAPSupport

I also used Multer but it didnt worked for me

entry.title: Building a docker image of a cds-dk app on a Raspberry Pi crash
entry.content:
Hello, I'm trying to containerise my CAP app to deploy it on a raspberry.

I am able to build the Docker image on windows without any problem.

But, when I try to build on the Raspberry (running Raspberry Os Lite), it gives this crash:

 

133.8 npm error code 132
133.8 npm error path /usr/src/app/node_modules/@sap/cdsdk/node_modules/@sap/hana-client
133.8 npm error command sh -c node checkbuild.js
133.8 npm error command failed
133.8 npm error Illegal instruction (core dumped)
133.8 npm notice
133.8 npm notice New patch version of npm available! 10.8.2 -> 10.8.3
133.8 npm notice Changelog: https://github.com/npm/cli/releases/tag/v10.8.3
133.8 npm notice To update run: pm install -g npm@10.8.3
133.8 npm notice
133.8 npm error A complete log of this run can be found in: /root/.npm/_logs/2024-09-25T11_34_13_5742-debug-0.log

 

 Any help is appreciated !

entry.title: ETIMEDOUT for SAP CAP App on BTP with external Postgres DB
entry.content:
Hi all,

we have a SAP CAP based application running on BTP CF. We are using an external Postgres DB for storing our data. This Postgres DB runs in a docker container which on a VM hosted on a dedicated HETZNER server.

From time to time (~10-60 mins) we see TCP connection timeouts in the SAP CAP backend like this:

 

2024-09-16T07:41:56.145+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [odata] - POST /odata/v4/api/fawkes/$batch
2024-09-16T07:41:56.150+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [odata] - > currentUser currentUser()
2024-09-16T07:41:56.150+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [user_service] - Instantiated User Service
2024-09-16T07:41:56.150+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [user_service] - getOrCreateUser --> Start
2024-09-16T07:41:56.150+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [user_service] - Checking DB for employee with id 3229
2024-09-16T07:43:46.793+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT [2024-09-16T07:43:46.791Z] [31mERROR[39m    Error: read ETIMEDOUT
2024-09-16T07:43:46.793+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at TCP.onStreamRead (node:internal/stream_base_commons:217:20)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.793+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDOUT at TCP.callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:128:17)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.795+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR [cds] - ️Uncaught Error: read ETIMEDOUT
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at TCP.onStreamRead (node:internal/stream_base_commons:217:20)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at TCP.callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:128:17) {
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR errno: -110,
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR code: 'ETIMEDOUT',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR syscall: 'read'
2024-09-16T07:43:46.796+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR }
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR [cds] - Error: read ETIMEDOUT
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at /home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/pg/lib/client.js:526:17
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async Object.all (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@cap-js/postgres/lib/PostgresService.js:174:26)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async PostgresService._checkCollation (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@cap-js/postgres/lib/PostgresService.js:97:21)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async Promise.all (index 1)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async PostgresService.begin (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@cap-js/db-service/lib/common/DatabaseService.js:70:7)
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR at async PostgresService._begin [as dispatch] (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-tx.js:199:3) {
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR errno: -110,
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR code: 'ETIMEDOUT',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR syscall: 'read',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR query: "SELECT collname FROM pg_collation WHERE collname = 'en-x-icu';\n ^",
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR id: '1397371',
2024-09-16T07:43:46.806+0000 [APP/PROC/WEB/0] STDERR level: 'ERROR',

2024-09-16T07:43:53.881+0000 [PROXY/0] STDOUT Exit status 137

 

On Postgres Side the log looks like this (don't be confused for the timestamps not matching):

 

2024-09-13 16:25:17.775 UTC [239] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out
2024-09-13 16:25:17.775 UTC [238] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out
2024-09-13 16:46:02.959 UTC [375] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out
2024-09-13 16:47:08.495 UTC [391] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out
2024-09-13 16:47:08.495 UTC [392] LOG:  could not receive data from client: Connection timed out

 

There is no explicit firewall configured and the connection to the database itself works - at least for a certain amount of time.

For testing purposes, I subscribed to the BTP Postgres service and created a binding to the application. In this setup, I don't have any connection issues.

I have already tried these things:

  • Using the same Postgres DB version in my own setup as the DB which is provisioned in BTP
  • Use the same (or almost the same) configuration of the database in my own setup as the BTP Postgres DB
  • Tried two different locations for providing my own postgres db

So the question is: Why do I have these connection timeouts? I'm a bit out of ideas what to try next so any help is really appreciated!

Thanks
Andreas
